prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: THETA dominates across all dimensions (trend slope error: 0.3166, seasonality correlation: 0.7645, RMSE: 21108), while ETS closely follows as a strong second. High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 0.69) and high seasonality variance (1.09) indicate models capture patterns very differently, making dba_combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "THETA",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "NaiveSeasonal"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "DWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ARIMA"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_rf", "catboost", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6942,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "THETA dominates across all dimensions (trend slope error: 0.3166, seasonality correlation: 0.7645, RMSE: 21108), while ETS closely follows as a strong second. High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 0.69) and high seasonality variance (1.09) indicate models capture patterns very differently, making dba_combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. THETA dominates as trend_champion (RMSE: 21972.8) and seasonality_champion (SMAPE: 0.06636), while ETS closely follows (RMSE: 21926.9). Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seas_corr variance (1.09) and model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 0.69). With only n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate with shrinkage≥0.35 for stability. RMSE spread between best (ETS: 21927) and worst (DWT_svr: 39215) is ~44%, justifying inverse_rmse_weights as a complementary approach."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding or are baselines. n_windows=3 meets threshold for preferring weighted candidates. Diversity is sufficient (≥2 different types, no ≥4 of same type). Top universe candidates (best_single_by_validation, ridge_stacking_*) have lower scores than current best (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 score 0.8017 vs best_single 0.7529). No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician:
{
  "add_names": ["best_single_by_validation"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Universe leaderboard shows best_single_by_validation (RMSE=23290.95) significantly outperforms current candidates (best RMSE=24655.86). n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong per-horizon selection benefit. With n_windows=3, adding one high-performing selection candidate improves the set without overfitting risk. Current set already has good diversity (selection, weighted, baseline types) so no removals needed.",
  "changes": ["added best_single_by_validation for superior RMSE from universe leaderboard"],
  "when_good": "candidate set includes at least one non-baseline selection method with n_unique_winners >= 3"
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: THETA dominates across all dimensions (trend slope error: 0.3166, seasonality correlation: 0.7645, RMSE: 21108), while ETS closely follows as a strong second. High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 0.69) and high seasonality variance (1.09) indicate models capture patterns very differently, making dba_combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. THETA dominates as trend_champion (RMSE: 21972.8) and seasonality_champion (SMAPE: 0.06636), while ETS closely follows (RMSE: 21926.9). Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seas_corr variance (1.09) and model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 0.69). With only n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate with shrinkage≥0.35 for stability. RMSE spread between best (ETS: 21927) and worst (DWT_svr: 39215) is ~44%, justifying inverse_rmse_weights as a complementary approach.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding or are baselines. n_windows=3 meets threshold for preferring weighted candidates. Diversity is sufficient (≥2 different types, no ≥4 of same type). Top universe candidates (best_single_by_validation, ridge_stacking_*) have lower scores than current best (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 score 0.8017 vs best_single 0.7529). No changes needed.

statistician.rationale: Universe leaderboard shows best_single_by_validation (RMSE=23290.95) significantly outperforms current candidates (best RMSE=24655.86). n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong per-horizon selection benefit. With n_windows=3, adding one high-performing selection candidate improves the set without overfitting risk. Current set already has good diversity (selection, weighted, baseline types) so no removals needed.
changes: added best_single_by_validation for superior RMSE from universe leaderboard
statistician.when_good: candidate set includes at least one non-baseline selection method with n_unique_winners >= 3
